The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかはししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTakahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{ill|lt=November 18|Q3027|WD=Q3027|1=November 18}} |神事 = Hōtō-shiki (ceremonial knife rite) }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hachijō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]] [[Category:qq]]

Approach |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 4.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 7.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Nara city |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = trad. mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = Kasuga-zukuri |別名 = Takahashi-san |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}{{ill|lt=February 19|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shunchū-sai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}
{{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{ill|lt=November 18|Q3027|WD=Q3027|1=November 18}} |神事 = Hōtō-shiki (ceremonial knife rite) }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hachijō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]]

Jinja (Nara)}} [[JA:高橋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24899217}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses||{{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine in Mishima, Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}|Takahashi Shrine (Mishima)}} {{神社 |名称 = Takahashi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takahashi jinja torii.JPG|270px]]
Approach |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 40 | 緯度秒 = 4.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 7.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-29 | 地図 = Nara city |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = trad. mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = Kasuga-zukuri |別名 = Takahashi-san |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}{{ill|lt=February 19|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shunchū-sai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}
{{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{ill|lt=November 18|Q3027|WD=Q3027|1=November 18}} |神事 = Hōtō-shiki (ceremonial knife rite) }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hachijō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:日本の食文化|神]]

If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Japanese cuisine]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = trad. mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = Kasuga-zukuri |別名 = Takahashi-san |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}{{ill|lt=February 19|Q2340|WD=Q2340|1=February 19}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shunchū-sai|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=March 11|Q2398|WD=Q2398|1=March 11}}
{{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}{{ill|lt=November 18|Q3027|WD=Q3027|1=November 18}} |神事 = Hōtō-shiki (ceremonial knife rite) }} '''Takahashi Shrine''' (たかはしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hachijō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} with former rank {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Two deities are enshrined, both regarded as founder deities of cuisine and clan deities of the Takahashi no Omi: :{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (イワカムツカリノミコト) :{{ill|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (タチハタチヂヒメノミコト) === History === The origin of Takahashi Shrine is unclear, but it appears certain that a place name "Takahashi" existed in this area in antiquity. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, an epidemic spread and many people died. The emperor, grieving, saw {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} in a dream saying, "This epidemic is my will. If Ōtataneko worships me, the curse will disappear and the country will be at peace." The emperor then found Ōtataneko and had him worship Ōmononushi as priest, and the epidemic ceased as foretold. In this ritual, a person named Ikuto from Takahashi village in Soekami District, Yamato, was appointed as sake-offering attendant to present sacred sake. Also, according to the entry for {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 53 in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},' when Keikō toured the east and reached {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}, he obtained a white clam while crossing a sea route. The attendant deity prepared it as namasu and offered it, and the emperor greatly praised its taste, granting him the title of Kashiwade no Omi. Ten generations later, the title Takahashi no Ason was additionally granted. The ancestor of this Takahashi no Ason (Kashiwade no Omi) was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iwakamutsukari-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and descendants hereditary-held Naizen duties for imperial meals; later they came to be worshiped as deity of cuisine. The change of surname from Kashiwade to Takahashi is thought related to this place name. The former site was about 100 m north of the current location, but is said to have burned in warfare by {{ill|lt=Matsunaga Hisahide|Q1143038|WD=Q1143038|1=Matsunaga Hisahide}} in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era and moved to the present site. Later, three sacred mirrors were unearthed at the old site and are enshrined as shrine treasures. === Rites === [[File:高橋神社 月次祭.jpg|150px|thumb|Takahashi Shrine monthly rite]] === Hōtō-shiki === Every May, wishing prosperity of people bearing the Takahashi surname nationwide and advancement in culinary skills, the shrine performs "Hōtō-shiki," demonstrating the classical knife tradition {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkusa-ryū Hōchōdō|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.sankei.com/article/20170515-SNG6BXYEIVOGBIHPANADTLZJRM/ "Hōtō-shiki" at Nara Takahashi Shrine; chief priest demonstrates elegant handling - Sankei News] viewed April 9, 2018. The rite existed from old times but had lapsed; from 2015 it was revived as a shrine ceremony. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}5-338 Hachijō ; Access {{underline|Rail}} * Nearest station: get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|Q6414371|WD=Q6414371|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Nara Kōtsū|Q11445000|WD=Q11445000|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} bus bound for Koinokubo (about 15 min), get off at Daianji Nishi 2-chōme (about 3 min walk) === References === * Daianji local gazetteer * Ichikojin Complete Preservation Edition "1000 Japanese Family Names and Crests" ISBN 978-4-584-20551-8 === Notes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takahashi Shrine|Q11670742|WD=Q11670742|1=Takahashi Shrine (Q11670742)}} * {{ill|lt=Takabe Shrine|Q11669186|WD=Q11669186|1=Takabe Shrine}} === External links === * {{Facebook|takahashijinjya|Takahashi Shrine}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takahashi Shrine (Q24899217)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:高橋氏|社たかはししんしや]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Japanese cuisine]]
